title: Mark Henry Brown
description: Canadian World War II flying ace
extract: Wing Commander Mark Henry Brown,  (9 October 1911 – 12 November 1941) was a Canadian-born flying ace who served with the Royal Air Force (RAF) during the Second World War. Credited with having shot down at least fifteen German aircraft, he was the first Canadian pilot to become a flying ace during the war. He was killed in action in November 1941 over Sicily.
